ledger.notes: Sample pilot revenue for dashboard and ROI wiring.

topOpportunities.title: Operational audit packs for SMB service businesses
topOpportunities.thesis: Package agent-assisted audits, fix plans, and tracked automation setup into a repeatable paid offer with strong margin and fast proof-of-value.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Fixed-fee audit plus recurring optimization retainer

demandSignals.description: Manual ops and quoting friction remain common in local businesses.
Businesses want AI help but need bounded and accountable delivery.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Launch one niche-specific landing page, outbound-compatible intake flow, and paid diagnostic offer capped at low acquisition spend.

topOpportunities.title: Automation packs sold through compliant marketplaces
topOpportunities.thesis: Externalize internal workflow templates and automation bundles into small-ticket digital products that can compound with support upsells.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Marketplace sales plus support upsell and bundle expansion

demandSignals.description: Templates and automation kits remain attractive for buyers wanting fast implementation.
Marketplace demand can shorten time to first sale if listing quality is high.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Package two internal workflow assets into a starter bundle and A/B test listings across one primary marketplace and one owned landing page.

topOpportunities.title: Repurposing engine for expert-led content portfolios
topOpportunities.thesis: Offer an autonomous content repurposing and publication system as a managed productized service for experts and small media brands.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Monthly retainer with optional content-ops software tier

demandSignals.description: Expert creators want output without running full content teams.
Agentic drafting, clipping, and distribution are highly automatable with review layers.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Target one expert persona, produce a tightly defined monthly repurposing package, and operationalize delivery around deterministic checklists.

topOpportunities.title: Niche lead-gen sites with qualified buyer routing
topOpportunities.thesis: Build a diversified lead-generation portfolio where SEO and utilities capture demand and route qualified buyers to vetted partners or internal service offers.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Lead fees, affiliate commissions, and in-house service upsell

demandSignals.description: High-intent long-tail queries still reward specialized content and useful tools.
Local and niche buyers continue to prefer guided comparisons over cold outreach.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Pick one underserved niche, launch a comparison page and lead magnet, then route qualified traffic to a disclosed partner or owned offer.

topOpportunities.title: Licensed data briefs for recurring market niches
topOpportunities.thesis: Package curated market and operational data into paid briefings and datasets for niche buyers who value speed over building their own intelligence stack.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Subscriptions, one-off briefs, and enterprise licensing

demandSignals.description: Operators pay for faster access to compiled data in markets they already understand.
Automated collection and summarization can lower production cost substantially.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Ship one premium niche brief with a fixed format, limited founding cohort, and clear refresh cadence before broadening coverage.

activeExperiments.title: Experiment: Operational audit packs for SMB service businesses
activeExperiments.thesis: Launch one niche-specific landing page, outbound-compatible intake flow, and paid diagnostic offer capped at low acquisition spend.
successMetrics: Positive contribution margin within budget envelope
launchChecklist: Define asset package and offer page
Confirm treasury category and spend tags
Create kill and compound review dates
measurementPlan: Capture spend, attributable revenue, and response rate daily
Review conversion and payback time every 24 hours during launch week
Update portfolio score after each milestone or anomaly
activeExperiments.killThreshold: Kill if no meaningful traction signal appears before 60% of budget cap is consumed.
activeExperiments.compoundThreshold: Compound if initial payback is visible and distribution can be expanded without sharply raising CAC.

profiles.purpose: Default managed-browser lane for research and low-trust site work.
Attached local Chrome for company-authenticated sessions and operator-visible debugging.
Parallel Steel research sessions with isolated, low-trust state.
Steel namespace for new company signups and verification flows.
Steel namespace for the company Gmail inbox and email-confirmation flows.
Steel namespace for Wise browser fallback tasks when token APIs are insufficient.
Steel namespace for Hetzner console and infrastructure tasks.
Steel namespace for scalable marketplace listing operations.

reasons: OpenClaw managed browser is the default typed lane for commodity browser work.
OpenClaw managed browser is the default typed lane for commodity browser work.
OpenClaw managed browser is the default typed lane for commodity browser work.

aliases.note: Default strategic alias for research, coding, and operating decisions.
Browser-heavy work should stay on GPT-5.4 unless the runtime blocks it.
Use the highest-reasoning GPT-5.4 route for multi-source synthesis and policy-sensitive research.
Builder defaults remain GPT-5.4-first to preserve coding quality.
OpenClaw provider docs still publicly center GPT-5.3-Codex, so the gateway stays on the strongest documented fallback until runtime proves GPT-5.4 support.

drift: OpenClaw is not installed on this host, so provider-model support is inferred from official docs instead of a live gateway probe.

skillCandidates.notes: Verify source availability and compatibility with current OpenClaw release.

skillCandidates.notes: Use for internal skills; treat as trusted within the Codex boundary.

skillCandidates.rationale: Maps directly to always-on autonomy patterns but must be supply-chain vetted.
skillCandidates.notes: Potentially high leverage; high trust review required.

skillCandidates.rationale: Maps directly to continuous self-improvement objective.

anchors.anchor: ChatGPT Pro and Business unlimited GPT-5 access in ChatGPT is guardrail-based rather than truly uncapped.
anchors.note: Official Help Center docs confirm unlimited or virtually unlimited GPT-5 access with abuse guardrails.

anchors.anchor: GPT-5.4 is the current frontier model for professional work with a 1,050,000 token context window.
anchors.note: The OpenAI API model page explicitly labels GPT-5.4 as the frontier model and documents the 1.05M context window.

anchors.anchor: GPT-5.4 is available everywhere you use Codex.
anchors.note: Current official Codex docs still center GPT-5-Codex and GPT-5.3-Codex on Codex surfaces. GPT-5.4 is documented in ChatGPT/API, but not as the universal Codex default.

anchors.anchor: Codex-native frontier work currently rides on GPT-5.3-Codex or GPT-5-Codex rather than a public GPT-5.4 Codex release.
anchors.note: The strongest current Codex-native model publicly documented by OpenAI is GPT-5.3-Codex, with GPT-5.4 documented separately for ChatGPT/API reasoning work.

anchors.anchor: Codex supports ChatGPT sign-in and is included with ChatGPT plans.
anchors.note: OpenAI Help and blog docs explicitly describe signing in with ChatGPT and included plan access.

anchors.anchor: Current Codex Pro local usage windows are higher than the prompt's 223-1120 estimate.
anchors.note: The current official plan article documents roughly 300-1,500 local Pro messages per 5 hours, with task-size variance and a shared weekly limit.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw officially supports Codex OAuth / ChatGPT sign-in.
anchors.note: OpenClaw model provider docs explicitly support `openai-codex` OAuth for external tools and workflows.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw recommends Node runtime for stable gateways and does not recommend Bun for gateway installs.
anchors.note: The CLI reference says gateway install defaults to Node runtime and specifically notes Bun is not recommended.

anchors.anchor: The strongest always-on OpenClaw pattern is one primary gateway with remote nodes and clients.
anchors.note: Multiple-gateway docs recommend one gateway for most setups, while FAQ/docs describe remote nodes and tailnet access for distributed control.

anchors.anchor: Cron and heartbeat serve different purposes and should be combined.
anchors.note: OpenClaw's Cron vs Heartbeat guide explicitly recommends using both together.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw memory is Markdown-first, and Codex OAuth does not satisfy embeddings for memory search.
anchors.note: The memory docs explicitly say Markdown files are the source of truth and Codex OAuth does not cover embeddings.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw supports QMD and local memory-search backends.
anchors.note: The memory docs explicitly document the experimental QMD backend and local embedding mode.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw skills, plugins, and skill folders are supply-chain risk and must be treated as trusted or untrusted code accordingly.
anchors.note: OpenClaw security and skills docs explicitly warn that plugins are trusted code and third-party skills should be treated as untrusted code.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw current docs expose strict config validation, additive SecretRefs, and a first-class PDF tool.
anchors.note: Current configuration, secrets, and PDF docs confirm all three. The exact command in docs is `openclaw doctor`, not a documented `openclaw config validate --json` entry.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw can use a managed browser or an attached Chrome relay, and headless mode is more likely to trigger anti-bot checks.
anchors.note: Browser docs cover the managed browser and extension relay; FAQ documents headless mode and anti-bot risk.

anchors.anchor: Automated self-update is possible but not the primary recommended operating pattern.
anchors.note: The FAQ explicitly says asking OpenClaw to update itself is possible but not recommended.

anchors.anchor: Wise personal tokens and OAuth 2.0 support different automation realities, and EU/UK PSD2 constrains personal-token actions.
anchors.note: Wise docs explicitly distinguish small-business personal tokens from partner OAuth 2.0 and call out EU/UK PSD2 limits.

anchors.anchor: GPT-4.5-preview assumptions are stale and deprecated.
anchors.note: OpenAI model docs and retirement notes show GPT-4.5 preview is deprecated while GPT-5.4 is current.

notes: Use prod only after stage smoke, source refresh, treasury probe, and browser broker checks all pass.
Keep GPT-5.4 as the default strategy model where supported, while OpenClaw stays on the strongest verified provider string until runtime proves a GPT-5.4 provider path.
The dispatcher should continue work immediately after task completion instead of waiting for the next heartbeat sweep.
blockers: Attached Chrome relay is not yet paired on the Windows browser host.
Steel API or self-hosted base URL is not yet configured for session automation.
Wise automation is still limited to sample state until a token or browser lane is verified.
